name: Topic Overview - CRISPR Gene Editing Applications

inputs.research_topic: CRISPR-Cas Gene Editing Applications in Human Therapeutics
inputs.keywords: CRISPR-Cas9, CRISPR-Cas12, base editing, prime editing, gene therapy, clinical trials, off-target effects, delivery systems, ex vivo editing, in vivo editing, germline vs somatic, ethical considerations
inputs.additional_information: Review scope: Focus on therapeutic applications of CRISPR gene editing in humans, covering period 2020-2025. Include: (1) Clinical trial progress and outcomes for diseases (sickle cell, beta-thalassemia, cancer immunotherapy, inherited retinal diseases), (2) Technical advances in delivery methods (AAV vectors, lipid nanoparticles, viral vectors), (3) Safety considerations and off-target effect mitigation strategies, (4) Regulatory landscape and approval pathways (FDA, EMA), (5) Ethical debates around germline editing and access equity. Identify 15-25 key publications from high-impact journals, 3-5 major research themes, emerging trends in precision editing (base editors, prime editors), research gaps in delivery efficiency and tissue-specific targeting, and controversies around clinical translation timelines. Synthesize into comprehensive overview suitable for grant proposal background or research strategy development.
